top_categories.workout_context: 75 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
BalatonFondo's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
Race-pace efforts that mimic BalatonFondo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of BalatonFondo. Train it until it feels boring.
Base fitness for BalatonFondo. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
At BalatonFondo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
BalatonFondo's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 75 miles.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mixed terrain at tempo for 75 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what BalatonFondo's mixed terrain actually does to you.
2-minute all-out efforts on mixed terrain. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.

race_overlay.heat: May in Lake Balaton, Hungary can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before BalatonFondo. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for BalatonFondo’s 75 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 75 miles of mixed terrain in Lake Balaton, Hungary.
